[PDF_PAGE 2 | score=5] How To Use This Book The new Monstrous Compendium format was designed with the Dungeon Master in mind. Monster entries are given in alphabetical order, printed on looseleaf sheets that can be organized for convenience. The sheets needed for an adventure can be placed in a separate folder for play, then later returned to the master binder. New monsters can be easily placed in the master binder. All monsters described here are typical for their type; likewise, the given encounter tables are guidelines for general play. DMs should note that unusual variations are encouraged, but are most effective when they depart from the expected. Each monster is described fully, with entries that describe be
[PDF_PAGE 7 | score=4] Waterborne, Salt water 2 Lyc., seawolf 3 Dragon turtle 4 Nymph 5 Warship, large é Warship, small 7 Pirates, small boats 8 Pirates, warship 9 Fishermen 10 Merchant ship, small 11 Merchant ship, small 12 Merchant ship, large 13 Warship, small 14 Koalinth 15 Manticora or nymph 16 Kapoacinth or lacedon 17 Troll, scrag, 18 Sea snake 19 DM Special 20 DM Special Waterborne, Fresh water 2 Spider, water 3 Spider, water 4 Lizard man 5 Lizard man 6 Navigational hazard 7 Merrow 8 Nymph 9 Fisherman 10 Fisherman 11 Merchant ship, small 12 Merchant ship, small 13 Warship, small 14 Koalinth 15 Pirates 16 Kapoacinth or lacedon 17 Troll, scrag 18 Sprite 19 DM Special 20 DM Special City or Town, Day Press gang
[PDF_PAGE 45 | score=3] Air/Earth Elementals Air Earth DIET: Air INTELLIGENCE: Low (5-7) TREASURE: Nil Nil ALIGNMENT: Neutral Earth, metal, or gem Low (5-7) Neutral SPECIAL ATTACKS: SPECIAL DEFENSES: See below +2 weapon or better to hit See below +2 weapon or better to hit MAGIC RESISTANCE: Nil Nil SIZE: L to H (8' to 16’ tall) L to H (8' to 16’ tall) MORALE: 8-12 Hit Dice: Champion 8-12 Hit Dice: Champion (15-16) (15-16) 16 Hit Dice: Fanatic (17) 16 Hit Dice: Fanatic (17) XP VALUE: 8 Hit Dice: 3,000 8 Hit Dice: 2,000 12 Hit Dice: 7,000 12 Hit Dice: 6,000 16 Hit Dice: 11,000 Air elementals can be conjured in any area of open air where gusts of wind are present. The common air elemental appears as an amorphous, shif
[PDF_PAGE 6 | score=2] Inhabited or Patrolled Areas Temperate Plain or Scrub Temperate Forest Temperate Rough or Hillf 2 Wilderness Table 2 Elf, grey 2 Elf, wood (gnome 10%) 2 Wyvern or dragon, black 3 Wilderness Table 3 Wyvern (gold dragon 10%) 3 Wyvern or dragon, green 3 Lyc., wolf or wolfwere 4 Patrol 4 Bear, brown 4 Lynx, giant or Lyc., tiger 4 Bear, brown 5 Patrol or NPC party 5 Spider, large 5 Bear, brown 5 Snake, pois. or spitting 6 Pilgrim or traveller 6 Jackal (jackalwere 10%) 6 Horse, wild or worg 6 Lion, mountain 7 Freeman or peasant 7 Boar, wild 7 Owl, giant or skunk, giant 7 Horse or herd animal 8 Freeman or peasant 8 Dog, wild or worg 8 Wolf 8 Gnome 9 Patrol 9 Wolf 9 Tribesman or NPC party 9 Tribesma
[PDF_PAGE 24 | score=2] Centipede DIET: Carnivorous INTELLIGENCE: Non- (0) TREASURE: Nil ALIGNMENT: Neutral Carnivorous Non- (0) Nil Nil Neutral Carnivorous Animal (1) Neutral SPECIAL ATTACKS: Poison SPECIAL DEFENSES: Nil MAGIC RESISTANCE: Nil SIZE: Tiny (1') MORALE: Unsteady (S-7) XP VALUE: 35 Giant centipedes are loathsome, crawling arthropods that arouse almost universal disgust from all intelligent creatures (even other monsters). They are endemic to most regions. One of the things which makes the centipede so disagreeable is its re- semblance to a worm. Its long body is divided into many segments from which protrude many tiny feet. Hence the name “centipede” (or hundred footed). The giant centipede is so calle
[PDF_PAGE 49 | score=2] Elf level fighter/5th level mage; and a 4th level fighter/4th level mage/4th level thief. Finally, if over one hundred and sixty elves are encountered, they will be lead by a 6th level fighter/9th level mage, anda 6th level fighter/6th level mage/6th level thief. These two extremely powerful leaders will have two retainers each—a 4th level fighter/Sth level mage, and a 3rd level fighter/3rd level mage/3rd level thief. All of these higher level leaders are added to, not taken from the total number of elves encountered. Elven women are the equal of their male counterparts in all as- pects of warfare. In fact, some bands of elves will contain units of female fighters, who will be mounted on uni
[PDF_PAGE 50 | score=2] Genie Djinn DIET: Omnivore INTELLIGENCE: Omnivore Average to highly (8-14) TREASURE: Nil Nil ALIGNMENT: Chaotic good Neutral evil Low to very (5-12) Efreeti Omnivore Very (11-12) Nil Neutral (lawful evil) = e SPECIAL ATTACKS: See below See below SPECIAL DEFENSES: See below See below MAGIC RESISTANCE: Nil Nil SIZE: L (10%/2' tall) L (8'-11' tall) MORALE: Elite (13-14) Champion (15-16) XP VALUE: 2,000 4,000 Noble 6,000 The three varieties of genie described here come from the elemen- tal planes of Air, Earth, and Fire. Living there, among their own kind, they are organized into their own societies. These beings are sometimes encountered on the Prime Material plane and are often summoned specif
